relationship: University/Staff -> Collects general waste and sends to BMA
subject: The University, Staff
source_text: General waste is collected and sent to the Bangkok Metropolitan Administration (BMA) for disposal.

relationship: Selling recyclable waste -> Revenue returned to departments or central fund
subject: Selling recyclable waste
object: Revenue returned to the respective departments or the central fund
source_text: Recyclable waste is separated and sold to a contracted antique shop, with the revenue returned to the respective departments or the central fund.

relationship: Cleaned plastics and single-use items sent to N15 -> Used as refuse-derived fuel
subject: Cleaned plastics and single-use items sent to N15
source_text: Specialized waste streams are handled separately: cleaned plastics and single-use items are sent to N15 to be used as refuse-derived fuel

relationship: Electronic waste is repairable -> Donated to the Mirror Foundation
subject: Electronic waste is repairable
source_text: Electronic waste is either donated to the Mirror Foundation if repairable or disposed of via standardized methods if it is hazardous.

relationship: Housekeepers -> Collect, weigh, record, and move waste
subject: Housekeepers
source_text: Housekeepers collect waste from inside buildings, are supposed to weigh and record it, and then move it to external collection points or cages.

relationship: Scheduling specific pickups -> Avoid cluttering regular collection points
subject: Scheduling specific pickups
object: Avoid cluttering the regular collection points
source_text: Large items, such as furniture or construction debris, are not collected daily; departments must schedule specific pickups for these materials to avoid cluttering the regular collection points.

relationship: Timing mismatches between housekeepers and collection trucks -> Major logistical issue
subject: Timing mismatches between housekeepers and collection trucks
source_text: A major logistical issue arises from timing mismatches between housekeepers and collection trucks.

relationship: Trucks finishing early and housekeepers dumping late -> Waste left uncollected and overflowing
subject: Collection trucks finishing rounds around 3:00–4:00 PM, Housekeepers dumping waste at 5:00 PM
object: Waste to be left uncollected and overflowing until the next morning
source_text: Collection trucks often complete their rounds around 3:00–4:00 PM, but housekeepers finish their shifts and dump the final load of waste at 5:00 PM, causing waste to be left uncollected and overflowing until the next morning.

relationship: Rough handling during collection -> Cages damaged
subject: Rough handling during collection
source_text: and they are frequently damaged by rough handling during collection.

relationship: Convenience or lack of discipline -> Mixing waste
subject: Convenience, Lack of discipline
source_text: Despite having separate bins and general knowledge about recycling, students and staff often mix waste out of convenience or lack of discipline.

relationship: Housekeepers guess or fabricate numbers -> Data accuracy is unreliable
subject: Housekeepers guess or fabricate numbers
object: Data accuracy regarding waste weight is unreliable
source_text: Furthermore, data accuracy regarding waste weight is unreliable because housekeepers often guess or fabricate numbers rather than weighing the trash properly.

relationship: Developing tools or systems -> Weighing and data recording process easier and more accurate
subject: Developing tools or systems
object: Weighing and data recording process easier and more accurate
source_text: The interviewee suggests that the student’s project could focus on developing tools or systems to make the weighing and data recording process easier and more accurate for the staff.
